title: euphorbia geyeri
intro: Euphorbia geyeri is a rare and endangered succulent plant species found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and inflammation, due to its high concentration of latex containing euphorbon, a toxic compound that also exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been used by indigenous cultures for its health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as promoting wound healing and reducing pain. Euphorbia geyeri is often prepared into herbal remedies, such as salves, ointments, and tinctures, which are applied topically to affected areas to harness its medicinal properties.
